falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: h_A1_001: payload-bounded chunking/reference loading is sufficient to keep workflow-heavy orchestration viable
falsificationQueries: workflow component payload and journal limits
convex mutation transaction limits affecting workflow internals
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: The workflow component documents hard limits: step inputs/outputs total 1 MiB per workflow execution, and workflow state is bounded by mutation limits (16 MiB including workflow overhead). Convex also enforces function argument limits (Node actions: 5 MiB) and transaction read/write ceilings. These constraints imply chunking/ref-loading is necessary but not sufficient; state growth and per-step serialization still create hard failure modes.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: h_A1_003: workflow-first remains preferable because built-in durability/retry offsets complexity if determinism risk is managed operationally
falsificationQueries: workflow determinism change behavior for in-flight workflows
workflow retry defaults and parallelism caveats
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Workflow docs state meaningful implementation changes (added/removed/reordered steps) can trigger determinism violations for active workflows. Maintainer issue #35 further states changing much of anything while workflows are ongoing can make them fail. Also, retry behavior is not universally automatic (workflow action retries default off unless configured; scheduled actions are at-most-once and not auto-retried). This undercuts the claim that durability/retry benefits reliably offset operational complexity.
